cards.title: Selection of Risk
cards.content:
ΓÇó Anti-selection: Tendency of high-risk people to seek insurance more than healthy people.
ΓÇó Morbidity: Rate of sickness/disease in a specific population (crucial for pricing health policies).

cards.title: Pre-existing Disease (PED)
cards.content:
ΓÇó A condition diagnosed or treated prior to the policy start.
ΓÇó IRDAI guidelines mandate a maximum waiting period of 36 Months for covering PEDs (reduced from 48 months).

items.text: Initial Waiting Period: No claims payable for illnesses during the first 30 days (Accidents are covered from Day 1).
Specific Illnesses (e.g., Cataract, Hernia) usually have a 24-month waiting period.
Cosmetic surgery, self-inflicted injuries, and non-allopathic treatments (unless explicitly covered).

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Group Underwriting: Based on the "Law of Large Numbers". It assesses the risk of the whole group rather than individuals.
Underwriting Tools: Proposal form, Medical reports, and previous claim history are key tools.
Portability: Moving from one insurer to another without losing the waiting period benefits already served.
Moral Hazard: A situation where the insured person is careless or over-utilizes medical services simply because they have insurance.
Loadings: Extra premium charged if the applicant has higher risks (like obesity or hypertension).
